podcast.name: Cross-Clamp Chronicles
podcast.blurb: Stories from the history of cardiac surgery and anesthesia — the people, the breakthroughs, and the hard-won lessons that shaped how we care for hearts today.

episodes.title: Blood Transfusion
episodes.summary: The birth of blood transfusion — from early concepts to future directions in cardiothoracic surgery.

episodes.title: Norman Shumway and the Birth of Heart Transplantation
episodes.summary: A brief look into the life and career of Dr. Norman Shumway — from small beginnings in southeastern Michigan to being dubbed the Father of Heart Transplantation, a titan of medical history.

episodes.label: Pt 2
episodes.title: Milestones in Cardiac Surgery, Part 2: Building on Their Shoulders (1958–1980)
episodes.summary: The two decades that turned cardiac surgery into a mature specialty — selective coronary angiography and bypass grafting, durable mechanical valves, the homograft and pulmonary autograft, orthotopic heart transplantation, and the first total artificial hearts — and the parallel birth of cardiac anesthesia as a recognizable subspecialty.
notes: Authors: E. A. Hessel II, J. C. Songster, J. Bryant, C. Jelly, R. Sladen.
Traces the work of Sones, Grüntzig, Favaloro, Loop, Starr, Ross, Shumway, Barnard, Kantrowitz, DeBakey, and Cooley.

Anesthesia milestones: Lowenstein's high-dose opioid technique, Kaplan's nitroglycerin and PA-catheter contributions, Wynands on perioperative ischemia, and the first cardiac anesthesia fellowship under William Lell (MGH, 1971).
references: Swan HJC, Ganz W, et al. Catheterization of the heart in man with use of a flow-directed balloon-tipped catheter. N Engl J Med. 1970.
Lowenstein E, et al. Cardiovascular response to large doses of intravenous morphine in man. N Engl J Med. 1969.
Favaloro RG. Saphenous vein autograft replacement of severe segmental coronary artery occlusion. Ann Thorac Surg. 1968.

episodes.label: Pt 1
episodes.title: Milestones in Cardiac Surgery, Part 1: Before the Pump
episodes.summary: The foundational era — Souttar and Cutler's early valve attempts, Gross and the ductus, Blalock & Taussig, Bigelow's hypothermia, Gibbon's heart–lung machine, and Lillehei's cross-circulation — when surgeons first dared to enter the heart.
